title: Boat Rental in Bali: How to Choose the Right Charter in 2026
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in Bali depends on your group size, budget, and itinerary. Departures are typically from Benoa Harbour or Padang Bai, with prices starting from around 1,800,000 IDR for a half-day shared tour. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bali or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bali?

Prices range from 1,800,000 IDR for a shared tour to over 30,000,000 IDR for a luxury crewed yacht. A half-day private speedboat for up to eight guests costs around 4,500,000 IDR. For a full-day catamaran charter, budget from 12,000,000 IDR depending on the season and itinerary. You can browse options and book a charter online to compare verified listings.

Which boat should you rent?

The best boat type depends on your planned activities and group size. Here are the most popular choices for Bali waters.

  1. Speedboat or RIB, ideal for island hopping to Nusa Lembongan and Nusa Penida; accommodates up to eight guests; from 4,500,000 IDR half-day.
  2. Cabin cruiser or motor yacht, comfortable for day trips or overnight stays; sleeps four to six; for example this motor yacht starts at 18,000,000 IDR for a full day.
  3. Catamaran, stable and spacious, perfect for sunset cruises and groups of up to twelve; from 12,000,000 IDR full-day.
  4. Traditional phinisi, a wooden sailing vessel offering a unique Balinese experience; accommodates up to twenty guests; from 25,000,000 IDR for a day charter.

Where to set off in Bali

The main departure points are Benoa Harbour in the south, Padang Bai on the east coast, and Serangan Marina. Benoa Harbour is the largest, with easy access to the Bukit Peninsula and offshore islands. Padang Bai serves ferries to the Gili Islands and Lombok. Serangan Marina, near Sanur, offers calm waters and is close to the Serangan turtle conservation area. Other options include Amed for diving trips to Liberty Wreck, and Lovina in the north for dolphin watching. Distances are manageable; for example, Benoa to Nusa Lembongan is about 17 nautical miles.

What you need to know about licences and seasons

No licence is required to rent a skippered boat in Bali, but bareboat charters demand a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC). The operator will confirm exact requirements before departure. The peak season runs from June to August (dry season with steady south-east trade winds) and again from December to January. The rainy season (November to March) can bring squalls, so early morning starts are advisable.

Book morning departures in the wet season to avoid afternoon thunderstorms; the best anchorage for lunch is around Gili Tepekong off the east coast.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,800,000, 35,000,000 IDR per day
  • Peak months: June, August and December, January
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 on most yachts, 20+ on phinisi
  • Currency: Indonesian Rupiah (IDR)

Questions about boat rental in Bali

Can I rent a boat for a day trip to Nusa Penida?

Yes, many charters offer Nusa Penida itineraries, typically including a visit to Kelingking Beach and Crystal Bay, with a journey of about 30 minutes from Padang Bai.

Is it possible to rent a luxury yacht for a wedding?

Several yachts like the Astondoa or Fairline models are available for events; contact the operator to arrange catering and decorations.

What is included in the rental price for a crewed boat?

Standard inclusions are skipper, fuel, crew, and sometimes soft drinks; food and harbour fees are often extra.

Do I need to tip the crew in Bali?

Tipping is appreciated but not mandatory; 5, 10% of the charter fee is customary if service is good.

Whether you choose a speedboat for island hopping or a catamaran for a sunset cruise, the rental process is straightforward. Boat owners in Bali are welcome to browse the listings and list their own vessel to reach a global audience. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bali and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
topic: how to choose a boat to rent in Bali
